summary:
The IQ range of 90–109 falls within one standard deviation of the mean (100) in a normally distributed score system, covering roughly 68% of the general population. It is classified as “average” because it represents typical cognitive function consistent across standardized assessment guidelines.

**Final Summary:**

Standardized classifications designate this interval as "average," aligning with established norms in psychology literature.
